summary: Deterministic closure plan for BuilderOS: remove false green, harden self-repair, synchronize truth artifacts, make PASS require commit/deploy proof, prove through the founder surface Adam actually uses, freeze Studio usability authority, and only then certify machine readiness.
post_completion_followups: Readiness/deploy proof scripts must treat /ready as a real fallback surface rather than stopping on the first timed-out ready endpoint.
The minimal founder_builder lane must expose truthful early /healthz and /ready responses before static/public middleware.
BuilderOS CLI proofs must load .env.builderos so the cheapest-capable worker lane is measured honestly instead of drifting to false missing-key blockers.

coder_must_not_decide: what counts as proof if the blueprint did not define it
whether a semantic answer is good enough when acceptance is soft
whether stale authority can be reused
whether a lesson discovered by ARC or SNT can remain unpromoted

non_goals: Do not widen into new product features.
Do not create a second active queue.
Do not upgrade FULLY_MACHINE_READY without final proof.
Do not manually patch around BuilderOS as the success path for this mission.

steps.title: Freeze architect handoff, SNT attack, and promotion contracts
required_edits: Architect handoffs must require both architecture output and machine-execution output.
SNT attacks must explicitly include founder-surface deception, transport truth, stale authority, and soft acceptance language.
Future promotion flow must route better answers into canonical authority instead of leaving them in chat.

must_prove: ARC cannot stop at high-level architecture only.
SNT attack categories are mandatory rather than optional.
Promotion of better answers is part of the governed path.

steps.title: Create one shared build-proof contract for founder/build actions
required_edits: Define one shared truth contract for code-changing build/repair actions.
A build/repair PASS must be illegal unless the contract records the exact commit SHA when commit transport is required.
The contract must distinguish local change, commit created, remote branch advanced, deploy queued, deploy live, and founder-runtime reproved.
If required proof is missing, the action must downgrade to FAIL or WAITING_FOR_PROOF rather than PASS.

must_prove: PASS without commit SHA is impossible for governed code-changing actions.
The proof contract has explicit states instead of one vague success flag.

steps.title: Wire remote-transport and deploy-sync truth into founder build results
required_edits: Founder build results must report exact commit SHA, remote branch proof, and deploy state using the shared build-proof contract from BAC-000.
If origin/main does not move, the result must not claim PASS.
If Railway still serves an older deploy SHA, the result must explicitly say the fix is not live yet.
Founder build-job poll/readback must refresh pending transport proof from live deploy SHA and founder-surface evidence instead of relying on local git ancestry inside Railway.
Generic founder code builds must persist committed truth quickly and let founder build-job poll/readback own redeploy/proof promotion to final PASS.
Founder-triggered redeploy must be pinned to the exact commit SHA created by the build, not a generic latest-head rebuild.
Surgical founder HTML-comment builds must return pending live-marker proof first; founder poll/readback may promote to PASS only after deploy sync plus live marker verification.
Alpha battery must assert commit/deploy proof semantics on build probes instead of treating job PASS alone as enough.

must_prove: A founder build cannot hide behind PASS while remote transport failed.
Deploy drift is surfaced as a first-class blocker.

steps.title: Create one canonical BP completion helper
required_edits: Create a shared helper that distinguishes technical pass, founder pass, point-B-complete, and queue-runnable incomplete work.
The helper must not treat TECHNICAL_PASS as founder-complete.
The helper must export named functions that callers can share instead of duplicating completion logic.

must_prove: Point B mission with founder_usability_pass=false is not complete.
TECHNICAL_PASS can coexist with founder-incomplete.
SCRAPPED_SALVAGE items remain non-runnable.

steps.title: Rewire scheduler, runner, and readiness surfaces to the shared completion truth
required_edits: Replace local completion checks with the shared helper from BAC-001.
If Point B is technically passed but founder-incomplete, status must be awaiting founder alpha/usability, not queue complete.
Scheduler health must not report healthy idle or no_work while Point B is founder-incomplete.
Runner must not silently stop on Point B technical pass alone.
Any readiness/reporting surface that summarizes queue health must use the same shared semantics.

must_prove: False-green queue complete state is impossible while Point B remains founder-incomplete.
Queue status distinguishes technical pass from founder pass.
Status reason is explicit, not generic.

steps.title: Create one shared self-repair escalation policy
required_edits: Define one shared policy module for repair attempts and escalation stages.
Implement three attempts per stage before higher escalation.
Require lessons carry-forward on every attempt.
Require explicit research before the third attempt in a stage.
Require added model/authority consensus for escalated stages.
Replace hard-coded attempt limits and ad hoc escalation logic in callers.

must_prove: No bounded repair path stops at two attempts if the failure remains repairable.
All repair callers use the same shared escalation constants and rules.

steps.title: Persist repair lessons and research carry-forward between attempts
required_edits: Persist per-attempt context with prior_attempts_consulted, lessons_loaded, research_completed, consensus_participants, proposed_fix, and outcome.
Before any retry, load prior attempt context and attach it to the next attempt.
If carry-forward context is unavailable, return blocked instead of guessing.

must_prove: Retries are explainable and cumulative rather than stateless.
Escalated attempts can show the exact lessons and research they inherited.

steps.title: Convert improvement loop output into deterministic blueprint deltas
required_edits: Do not create a second active queue.
Change improvement output from advisory prose to deterministic blueprint-delta contracts.
Each improvement item must name the affected mission/step, authority owner, proof required, and whether it is auto-applicable or must return to ARC.
If an improvement changes runtime behavior, it must map to a governed mission artifact, not a floating recommendation.
When ARC or SNT finds a stronger answer, promotion into canonical authority must be required or explicitly rejected with reason and owner.

must_prove: Improvement outputs can be consumed by ARC/BPB without new interpretation.
No secondary work queue is introduced.

steps.title: Synchronize receipts, queue truth, objective verdicts, and readiness artifacts
required_edits: Create one sync helper for post-acceptance artifact updates.
Successful acceptance must update BP item truth, objective verdict, readiness report, and freshness metadata together.
Founder usability confirmation must update Point B completion truth through the same governed sync path.
Artifacts that are no longer fresh must be marked stale rather than left looking current.
Canonical product-home and file-manifest references for LifeRE founder usability authority must be updated through this same sync path.

must_prove: No major artifact can remain falsely fresh after related runtime truth changes.
Product readiness report reflects current registry breadth after regeneration.

steps.title: Tighten founder-UI alpha truth and Studio usability gating
required_edits: Fail on console errors unless an explicit allowlist path with named justification exists.
Do not let ready_for_founder_alpha imply point_b_complete.
Founder usability must only become true through the explicit founder confirmation path.
Founder-facing receipts must say technical pass only when founder pass is still false.
Add the exact founder-surface failure that was observed live: generic knowledge/counsel prompts must not misroute into display/status theater.
Acceptance must preserve the distinction between API proof, scripted browser proof, and exact founder-surface proof.
Studio must freeze a founder usability contract covering response quality, conversation flow, responsiveness expectations, and adaptive surface behavior.
Soft semantic acceptance wording must be replaced or bounded by explicit corpus/schema criteria where possible.

must_prove: UI proof and queue proof do not drift apart.
Founder alpha readiness is distinguishable from founder alpha completion.

steps.title: Clean stale authority tags and harden legacy boundaries
required_edits: Replace stale amendment-first @ssot tags in the active autonomy spine with the canonical authority chain.
Ensure legacy queue runners and Hist-owned artifacts cannot be mistaken for active authority.
Add explicit hard-stop or history-only guardrails where legacy files remain present.

must_prove: Active runtime no longer references MISSION_QUEUE authority.
Cold agents are less likely to confuse legacy and active orchestration.

steps.title: Create closure acceptance and proof harnesses
required_edits: Create one acceptance command for this closure mission.
Create one founder-UI proof script that uses the real LifeOS founder path.
Create one same-tier determinism script that fails if a stronger model tier is used.
Create one build/deploy truth script that verifies commit SHA, remote branch movement, deploy SHA movement, and live reprobe.
Wire npm scripts for all of these.

must_prove: Closure acceptance can be run with one command.
UI proof and determinism proof are first-class citizens, not manual notes.
Build/deploy proof is machine-enforced rather than inferred from narrative.

steps.title: Run same-tier determinism and founder-UI sacrificial proof
required_edits: Run the same-tier determinism proof on a sacrificial blueprint slice.
Run the founder-UI proof through the real LifeOS founder surface.
Run explicit build/deploy proof on at least one sacrificial live repair/build flow.
If the system misses or lies, fix the system, not the receipt.
Write explicit PASS/FAIL receipts with blockers if any proof fails.

must_prove: Same-tier coder outputs are materially equivalent.
The founder UI can move between counsel and build paths honestly.
Repair/escalation behavior is visible and receipted.
Build success, remote transport success, and live deploy success are separately visible and cannot be conflated.

steps.title: Upgrade certification only if all proof gates pass
required_edits: Set FULLY_MACHINE_READY true only if closure acceptance, same-tier determinism, founder-UI proof, build/deploy truth proof, and Point B founder completion all pass.
If any proof is missing or failed, leave FULLY_MACHINE_READY false and record the blocker honestly.

must_prove: Certification state matches actual proof state.
Handoff status does not overclaim readiness.

ssot_change_receipt: Escalation policy hardened: shouldRunWebSearchBeforeAttempt added; web search wired before 3rd solo attempt (founder-build-self-repair.js); prior: V1 L0 truth boundary: build-proof-contract COMMIT_ONLY_NOT_LIVE fails closed.
